commit.message:
fix(desktop): upscale QR to 800x800 8-bit grayscale so it survives Signal's JPEG recompression

The 300x300 1-bit QR screenshots were unscannable after Signal re-compressed
them as JPEG when sending as attachments. Now we upscale with nearest-neighbor
sampling to 800x800 and output as 8-bit grayscale PNG, preserving sharp module
edges even after lossy recompression. Also bumped blank-detection threshold
from 2000 to 5000 bytes to match the larger output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add redirects for insurance category slugs at top level

Redirect /fr/assurance-auto → /fr/assurance/assurance-auto etc.
so both URL patterns work.

entry. Set <html lang> dynamically from getLocale() in root document.

Globe icon dropdown that lists available locales and navigates to the
localized version of the current path via full-page navigation so the
middleware can set the locale cookie.

commit.message:
fix: embedded ICMP fabric redirect after NAT rewrite (split-RG)

In split-RG cluster, ICMP Time Exceeded from transit routers arrives
on the WAN node but must reach the LAN host on the peer node.
handle_embedded_icmp_v4/v6() was calling try_fabric_redirect() BEFORE
NAT rewrite — the peer received packets with SNAT'd embedded headers
and couldn't match them to any session, silently dropping them.

Fix: when FIB fails (UNREACHABLE/BLACKHOLE) for the original client,
set META_FLAG_KERNEL_ROUTE instead of attempting immediate fabric
redirect. xdp_forward's KERNEL_ROUTE path re-FIBs using post-NAT
packet headers (outer dst rewritten to original client IP), detects
UNREACHABLE, and fabric-redirects with correct headers.

Also change meta_flags assignment from = to |= to preserve
KERNEL_ROUTE flag when setting META_FLAG_EMBEDDED_ICMP.

Tested: split-RG MTR shows all intermediate hops (was 100% loss),
make test-failover 14/14 pass at 7.86 Gbps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add plan levels (Quick Fix, Standard, Complex)

Add three levels of planning:

- Quick Fix: < 1 hour, 2 min to create
- Standard: 1-3 days, 5-10 min to create
- Complex: > 1 week, full checklist

This prevents the "too much paperwork" problem while ensuring
important features get proper planning.

commit.message:
feat: add comprehensive plan checklist to planning system

Update planning template with complete checklist covering:

- Database (migration, tables, indexes, performance)
- Security (validation, auth, secrets, injection)
- Error handling (Result types, logging, HTTP codes)
- Testing (unit, integration, affected code)
- Documentation (README, API, migration, env vars)
- Configuration (env vars, defaults, validation)
- Breaking changes (API, DB, config, migration)
- Performance (N+1, indexes, caching)
- Monitoring (logging, metrics, alerts)

Each category now includes a checklist and explanation of why it matters.

Fix empty homepage on Render: await dbReady before querying products

Server components query the DB at render time, but on Render the
auto-seed may not have completed yet. Now getFeaturedProducts,
getCategories, and getProducts await dbReady before querying,
ensuring seed data exists before the page renders.

commit.message:
fix(auth): call supabase.auth.signOut() on sign-out — clears Supabase session so AuthBridge stops re-firing SIGNED_IN

Without this, Google OAuth users could not sign out: clearAuth() removed the Zustand token
but the live Supabase session remained in the browser, causing AuthBridge.onAuthStateChange
to emit SIGNED_IN again and navigate back to /chat in a loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add USB CAT, USB audio monitor, and WSJT-X digital mode support

- CATTransport protocol abstraction for LAN and USB connections
- SerialCATConnection: USB CAT via Silicon Labs CP2102N at 115200 baud
- SerialPortScanner: enumerate available serial ports
- USB Audio Monitor: CoreAudio loopback from USB audio codec to Mac speakers with NR pipeline
- Configure Radio for WSJT-X: sends OM0D (USB-DATA) + MS002 (USB audio TX), posts system notification
- Revert to Voice Mode: restores previous operating mode + MS001 (microphone TX)
- LAN connect while digital mode active: auto-restores mode and switches TX audio to LAN (MS003)
- Unit tests for CAT command encoding and protocol parsing

commit.message:
Fix current lesson banner freezing at 00:00 when lesson ends

When the countdown reached zero, the interval kept running and calling
fetchCurrent() every second while the banner stayed visible at 00:00.
Now immediately hides the banner when time expires and checks for the
next lesson after a 3-second delay.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add trade protection gates, signal confirmation delay, and spread check

Trading logic:
- Min profit exit (0.6%) prevents selling at a loss unless sell confidence > 80%
- Min hold time (5m) blocks premature signal-sells
- Bid-ask spread check (0.3%) skips buys when spread is too wide
- Signal confirmation delay: 60% of trades wait 2 cycles for signal persistence
- Dynamic scalp cooldown: extends cooldown on repeated buy-sell cycles per pair
- All parameters are AI-tunable via strategy.json

Dashboard:
- Trade list no longer auto-scrolls, newest trades shown at top
- 6 new AI parameter sliders for the new trading gates
- AI params card visual improvements (accent border, header background)
- Fix stale "running" LLM status persisting across restarts
- Simplify LLM scheduler startup delay
- Dangerous commands (pause/sell/kill) now require confirmation dialogs
- Deploy script fixed for submodule-based repo structure

commit.message:
fix(onboarding): use fetchQuery instead of invalidateQueries on approve — prevents redirect loop

invalidateQueries marks cache stale but does not refetch when no active observer is mounted
(OnboardingGate is not rendered at /avatar-setup). Cache stays null, OnboardingGate redirects
back to /avatar-setup on navigate('/chat'). fetchQuery forces a real GET /avatars/me call and
populates the cache before navigation, so OnboardingGate sees the avatar immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: Fractal Folded Search paper artifact

Paper, source code, pre-computed tree snapshots, and benchmark script
for reproducing the compression table (Table 1). Five fold strategies
× four depth levels on a 1,554-node tree from the Ada Research project.

Contents:
- paper/ — full paper (markdown)
- src/ — types, 5 fold strategies, benchmark CLI
- data/ — pre-computed tree snapshots (5 strategies, depth 3)
- benchmark/ — raw compression results

Run `npm install && npm run benchmark` to reproduce Table 1.

commit.message:
feat: add work_from_date and comment fields to RulesConfigSidebar

The individual product rules sidebar was missing the two new fields
added in migration 025. Added date picker and textarea to match
the bulk CustomRulesModal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ui): add ListTransition component & fix __list reactive proxy (#926)

* feat(ui): add ListTransition component for animated list enter/exit [#905]

Add `ListTransition` — a keyed list reconciliation primitive with
CSS-driven enter/exit animations via `data-presence` attributes.

- Core runtime (`dom/list-transition.ts`): comment marker boundaries,
  keyed reconciliation, scope disposal on removal, deferred DOM removal
  during exit animations via `onAnimationsComplete()`
- Component wrapper (`component/list-transition.ts`): thin wrapper
  exposing `each`/`keyFn`/`children` props
- Fix: defer `onAnimationsComplete` until after DOM reconciliation so
  `getAnimations()` sees the element in the document
- Fix: make queryMatch data branch stable (remove `branch !== 'data'`
  exception) so ListTransition persists across data updates
- Update entity-todo example to use ListTransition with direct signal
  API access (`todosQuery.data.items`) for reactive list updates
- Update task-manager task-detail to use `refetch()` after mutations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(task-manager): use direct signal API access in task-detail queryMatch [#905]

The queryMatch data callback parameter (proxy) is not recognized as
reactive by the compiler. Use taskQuery.data.* directly so the compiler
generates reactive getters, fixing status transitions not updating the UI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(task-manager): move transitions computed to top level [#905]

Move the transitions array from inside the queryMatch data handler
to the top level of the component, where the compiler correctly
wraps it in computed(). Uses a null guard for when data hasn't
loaded yet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui): wrap __list items in reactive proxy for index-key updates [#905]

When __list uses index-based keys (compiler default for .map() without
explicit key prop), items at the same index could change identity after
a refetch. Previously, __list reused the stale DOM node without updating
its content. Now each item is wrapped in a signal-backed proxy — when
the item at a key changes, reactive bindings (domEffect, __child) inside
the node see the updated data automatically.

Same fix applied to listTransition for consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(task-manager): add key prop to transition buttons for correct reconciliation [#905]

The compiler generates static text bindings for .map() renderFn parameters
(since they're classified as non-reactive plain variables). With index-based
keys, __list reuses stale DOM nodes whose text never updates. Adding
key={tr.status} ensures each status transition gets a fresh node with
correct text content.

Also adds type="button" to fix biome a11y lint warnings.

* chore: update changeset to cover __list reactive proxy fix [#905]

* fix(task-manager): use data handler parameter to fix typecheck [#905]

The data handler was accessing taskQuery.data directly (possibly undefined)
instead of the handler's typed parameter (guaranteed non-undefined by
queryMatch). Use the proxy parameter for type safety.

* fix(task-manager): use taskQuery.data for reactive bindings in queryMatch [#905]

The previous fix used the queryMatch data handler parameter (`task`),
which the compiler classifies as a non-reactive plain variable — all
JSX bindings became static. Switch back to `taskQuery.data` (recognized
by the compiler as a signal API property) with a type guard for TS
narrowing. See #927 for the underlying compiler issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(compiler): replace reactive-vs-static with literal-vs-non-literal for JSX codegen [#927]

The compiler previously used static analysis to determine if an expression
was reactive and only wrapped reactive expressions in __child()/__attr()/getters.
This broke when reactive values flowed through function boundaries (callback
parameters, HOFs, proxy-backed objects).

Now the compiler only checks if an expression is a literal (string, number,
boolean, null). All non-literal expressions get reactive wrappers, and the
runtime (domEffect) handles actual tracking. Idle effects with no signal
dependencies have zero ongoing cost.

- Add isLiteralExpression() helper for literal detection
- transformChild/transformChildAsValue: non-literal → __child(() => expr)
- processAttribute: non-literal → __attr(el, name, () => expr)
- buildPropsObject: non-literal → get name() { return expr; }
- __list keeps reactive gate (static arrays don't need reconciliation)
- Remove stale usedFeatures logic from compiler.ts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(examples): use queryMatch handler parameters directly [#927]

Now that the compiler wraps all non-literal expressions reactively,
queryMatch data handler parameters work without workarounds.

- task-detail.tsx: use (task) => task.title instead of taskQuery.data.title
- todo-list.tsx: use (response) => response.items instead of todosQuery.data.items
- create-vertz-app template: use ListTransition with handler parameter
- Update e2e screenshot baselines

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ui-server): fix double-nesting signal collection in Fast Refresh [#721] (#928)

The factory wrapper and __$refreshPerform both called
startSignalCollection/stopSignalCollection. Since signal collection
uses a stack, the wrapper's inner collector captured all signals,
then __$refreshTrack discarded them during refresh mode. This left
__$refreshPerform with an empty signal array, so signal state was
never preserved across HMR cycles.

Fix: add a refreshSignals stash in __$refreshTrack so the wrapper's
collected signals are passed back to __$refreshPerform. Remove the
redundant outer signal collection from __$refreshPerform.

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add software engineering handbook

Complete workflow system for managing 25+ repositories with AI agents.

Documents:
- Project initialization (templates, structure)
- Feature development workflow (issue → release)
- Issue management (templates, labels, lifecycle)
- Pull request process (template, checklist, merge)
- Code review guidelines (checklist, feedback)
- Release process (semantic versioning, deployment)
- Documentation standards (README, code docs)
- AI agent integration (project context, prompts)
- Repository templates

Principle: Human-driven, AI-executed - humans decide WHAT,
AI does HOW. Consistency across all repositories through
standardized templates and rules.
